name: Adi Parva
summary: The origins of the Kuru line: Shantanu and Ganga, Bhishma's terrible vow, the births of Dhritarashtra, Pandu and Vidura, the fire-born Draupadi, the boyhood rivalry of the cousins, and the house of lac.

name: Sabha Parva
meaning: The Book of the Assembly Hall
summary: Yudhishthira's imperial sacrifice, Shishupala's death at Krishna's hand, and the dice game — Draupadi dragged into the hall, the vows of Bhima, and the exile of the Pandavas.

name: Vana Parva
summary: Twelve years in the forest: Arjuna wins the Pashupatastra from Shiva, Yudhishthira answers the Yaksha's questions, and the seeds of the war are watered by grief.

name: Virata Parva
summary: The thirteenth year in disguise at King Virata's court — Arjuna as the dance teacher Brihannala, Bhima in the kitchens, Draupadi as a maid — and the cattle raid that nearly unmasks them.

name: Udyoga Parva
summary: The last attempts at peace. Krishna rides as envoy to Hastinapura and shows his cosmic form to the blind court. Karna learns whose son he truly is — and keeps the secret.

name: Bhishma Parva
summary: The armies meet at Kurukshetra. Arjuna's bow slips from his hand — and Krishna sings the Bhagavad Gita. Ten days of Bhishma's command, until he falls on a bed of arrows.

name: Drona Parva
summary: Five days of slaughter under the teacher's command: the boy Abhimanyu trapped alone inside the wheel formation, Arjuna's terrible vow against Jayadratha, and a lie that fells Drona himself.

name: Karna Parva
summary: Two days of Karna's command, ending in the duel the whole epic has waited for — a chariot wheel sunk in the earth, a forgotten mantra, and brother slaying brother.

name: Shalya Parva
summary: The eighteenth day. Shalya falls to Yudhishthira, Shakuni to Sahadeva, and Bhima breaks Duryodhana's thigh at the lake — as vowed, and against the rules.

name: Sauptika Parva
meaning: The Book of the Sleeping Warriors
summary: Ashwatthama walks into the sleeping Pandava camp at night. By dawn the victors have no sons left. The brahmastra is turned on the unborn.

name: Stri Parva
summary: The women walk the field among the dead. Gandhari, who bore a hundred sons and lost them all, curses Krishna and his entire race.

name: Shanti Parva
summary: Yudhishthira, crowned and inconsolable, sits at the feet of the dying Bhishma, who teaches the dharma of kings from his bed of arrows.

name: Anushasana Parva
summary: Bhishma's final teachings — on giving, on conduct, on the thousand names of the divine — until, with the sun turning north, he lets go of his life.

name: Ashvamedhika Parva
meaning: The Book of the Horse Sacrifice
summary: The empire is re-consecrated with the horse sacrifice. Krishna revives the stillborn Parikshit — the last hope of the line.

name: Ashramavasika Parva
summary: Dhritarashtra, Gandhari and Kunti retire to the forest, and are taken at last by a forest fire — a quiet end for those the war spared.

name: Mausala Parva
summary: Gandhari's curse ripens. The Yadavas slaughter one another with reeds of iron, and Krishna, resting beneath a tree, is taken by a hunter's arrow.

name: Mahaprasthanika Parva
meaning: The Book of the Great Journey
summary: The Pandavas renounce the kingdom and walk north into the Himalaya. One by one they fall on the path — until only Yudhishthira and a dog remain.

name: Svargarohana Parva
meaning: The Book of the Ascent to Heaven
summary: Yudhishthira refuses heaven without the dog at his side, endures one final test of hell, and the wheel of the tale comes to rest.
